verificationLabel: Round 9 updated after Friday results on 1 May 2026
summary: Round 9 has started badly for the board, with the Bulldogs charity play losing to a Cowboys comeback and the Storm dropping a seventh straight game in a 26-10 loss to the Dolphins.

sources.label: Friday Night Lights: Bulldogs v Cowboys; Dolphins v Storm

scoreboard.note: AJ Brimson and Chris Randall are back for Gold Coast while Canberra remain light in the back row after the loss of Zac Hosking and Noah Martin.
North Queensland stormed back to win and the Bulldogs charity play missed despite looking like the cleaner pre-game read.
Parramatta stay unchanged while the Warriors get Adam Pompey back and list Luke Metcalf on the interchange.
Melbourne fell 26-10 at Suncorp and their losing streak has now stretched to seven straight.
Brisbane suddenly look a lot more dangerous with Walsh, Mam, Reynolds, Carrigan and Cory Paix all named.
Kalyn Ponga, Bradman Best, Greg Marzhew and Tyson Frizell all return, making Newcastle far more trustworthy than last week's version.
Cronulla stay largely settled while the Tigers continue to juggle their depth around the edges and bench.
Penrith roll in unchanged while Manly still have no Turbo and lose Corey Waddell from the side that beat Parramatta.

liveAlerts.text: The Bulldogs charity play has already gone down, so Round 9 opens with an immediate hit to the ledger.
The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger now sits at 2-2 and -$3.50 after the $10 Bulldogs miss.
The Cowboys comeback win is the kind of loss that has to be logged cleanly without triggering revenge betting.
The Storm have now lost seven straight and should be removed from any trust bucket until they prove otherwise.
Broncos, Knights and Panthers now carry more weight as the remaining key reads for the weekend.
The right move now is steady tracking, not trying to win the charity loss back live.
